data.title: Reddit: Execution Is Strong, But Growth Appears Priced In
data.description:
Introduction:

Reddit (NYSE:RDDT) posted strong first-quarter earnings, with growth seen across every important metric and category. With both active users and top-line growth surging, along with improved gross, operating, and net profitability, the company appears to be firing on all cylinders. I was also intrigued by the numerous product innovations they've released recently, along with continued penetration in the ever-important international markets.

Despite the company's incredible momentum, I believe that Reddit's current valuation is simply unsustainable, despite my optimistic assumptions regarding long-term revenue growth and improved profitability ratios. In this article, I break down Reddit's recent results, key financial drivers, and my discounted cash flow analysis of the company that suggests the stock is currently immensely overvalued despite the rock solid fundamentals.

Business Description & Catalysts:

Reddit is a globalized social media and content discovery platform that is known for its user-generated communities, called subreddits, that cover thousands of different topics.

The company primarily earns revenue by selling advertising space on its website and app, using advertising models such as cost-per-click (CPC), where advertisers only pay when a user clicks on their ad, cost-per-thousand impressions (CPM), where advertisers are charged based on the number of times the ad is shown, and fixed term delivery, where advertisers play a flat fee to have their ad show a set number of times.

This diversified approach gives advertisers the ability to tailor their advertising campaign to focus on various different visibility or engagement goals that best suit their product or service offerings.

Reddit is also making solid progress in ad product innovation, as they recently began embedding ads into comment sections on subreddits, which now make up 6% of total impressions on the platform. I believe that this suggests advertisers are finding value in integrated ad placements that don't directly disrupt the user experience, which likely translates to higher engagement.

Reddit reached 108.1 Daily Active Uniques (DAUq) in Q1 FY25, growing 31% YoY. Growth was driven by product improvements such as machine translations to 13 new languages and stronger visibility on external search engines like Google. Logged in DAUq users, which is Reddit's most monetizable customer base, grew 23% YoY to 48.7 million, which reflects strong engagement trends. Additionally, Weekly Active Uniques (WAUq) hit 401.3 million, up 31% YoY.

Reddit's average revenue per unique user (ARPU) grew 23% YoY to $3.63, which was in line with the growth rate in logged in DAUq users. This makes sense given that Reddit can more easily track these users' behavior and interest, which allows for better ad targeting. ARPU in the U.S. remains significantly higher than the figures seen in international markets, at $6.27 and $1.34 respectively.

However, I believe that we will begin to see emerging markets slowly close this gap over time for a couple of reasons. First off, in 2024 global advertising spend reached $1.1 trillion, which was a 7.3% increase YoY. Digital advertising accounted for the majority of this growth, and social media platforms specifically saw a 15% YoY increase in advertising spend.

This broader rise in both digital and social media ad spend creates a favorable backdrop for a platform like Reddit to expand its monetization efforts in non-domestic markets, which, I believe, will allow ARPU to grow internationally over time.

Additionally, emerging regions such as Southeast Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa, and Latin America are all experiencing significant digital infrastructure growth. Factors such as more affordable smartphones, broader internet access, and a younger population should drive increased adoption of social media platforms globally, which should provide another tailwind for Reddit's international ARPU.

Finally, Reddit earned $34 million from licensing content in the first quarter, which was up 66% compared to the year-ago period. These content agreements, such as the firms deal with Google to license user-generated posts for training LLMs, are now providing a secondary source of revenue for Reddit tied to Reddit's greatest asset: its user base. While this revenue is not as stable compared to advertising, I believe that licensing deals could become a more substantial portion of the top line if demand for AI training data remains strong over the coming years as the technology continues to proliferate.

Financials:

Reddit delivered first quarter revenues of $392.4 million, which was up 61% YoY. Advertising revenue accounted for ~$359 million of the top line, with data and licensing contributing the rest. The firm's gross margin expanded to 90.5%, which was up 190bps YoY and reflects improved operating efficiency.

Operating expenses declined drastically from $804.6 million in Q1 FY24 to $351.4 million this past quarter, largely due to a significant reduction in stock-based compensation (SBC). This allowed Reddit to report an enormous increase in net income, from a $575.1 million net loss a year ago to a positive figure of $26.2 million.

While a large portion of this improvement was driven by post-IPO adjustments to SBC, the significant turnaround still highlights the company's improving cost discipline and growing operating leverage. To provide further evidence, both SG&A and R&D expenses declined around 56% YoY.

Adjusted EBITDA rose sharply to $115.3 million in Q1, up from just $10 million a year ago. Reddit breaks down SBC and depreciation by department, which are both added back to adjusted EBITDA, and this gives investors a better idea of where this spend is actually taking place.

Some of the company's software development costs are capitalized, which provides a lift to their reported EBITDA figure. This makes sense given these investments typically lead to tangible returns, in the form of better targeted advertisements or enhanced features which allow Reddit to charge advertisers a higher price per ad placement.

Finally, FCF also experienced a major jump, rising from $29.2 million in Q1 FY24 to $126.6 million in Q1 FY25. Reddit's capital-light business model, where they have chosen to rely on third-party cloud providers rather than building out their own data center infrastructure, allows their capital expenditure budget to remain relatively small.

This makes FCF a reliable proxy for the company's ability to generate cash that can be used to reinvest in the business, strengthen its balance sheet, or perhaps eventually be returned to shareholders in the form of dividends or buybacks.

Risks:

While Reddit's Q1 results were incredibly strong, there are several important risk factors to consider that make me less optimistic.

First off, user engagement remains relatively low relative to some of their peers in the industry. Reddit's DAUq/WAUq ratio remained steady on a YoY basis in Q1 FY25 at 27%. This shows that only around a quarter of weekly users are visiting Reddit's site or app on a daily basis. By comparison, Meta reported a DAU/MAU ratio of 84% and Snapchat is over 52% when looking at the same metric.

To me, this suggests that Reddit's platform may be less habit-forming for people, which inherently lowers its attractiveness to advertisers who are looking to get their products in front of as many eyeballs as possible on a daily basis.

Another risk Reddit faces is that its business model is highly dependent on third-party search traffic (particularly from Google). Reddit's CEO acknowledged on their recent earnings call that traffic to their site could fluctuate based on changes to Google's search engine algorithm.

With the recent rollout of Gemini AI overviews, I think that users may begin to find answers to their questions directly in the search results without having to visit the Reddit site. This poses a risk to organic customer acquisition, especially for newer or casual users.

This risk is compounded by the rise of advanced LLMs such as ChatGPT, which are fantastic at finding answers to the kinds of questions users often use Reddit to find. As generative AI applications continue to improve, I believe that Reddit's role as a go-to source of knowledge may be replaced. Unless Reddit can find new ways to integrate or differentiate its user-generated content in the age of AI, this dynamic is likely to have a significant impact on engagement, new user acquisition, and most importantly, advertising revenue.

Valuation:

To determine Reddit's intrinsic value, I created a discounted cash flow model. The perpetual growth rate I used in the model was 3.5%, and my projection covers the next five years.

For my cost of capital, I first computed the cost of debt, which is negligible for Reddit. Next, I calculated the cost of equity using the yield on a 10-year U.S. treasury bond as a proxy for the risk-free rate, the beta from the average social media company listed in the U.S. (around 1.4), and the equity risk premium from Professor Damodaran's website. Below are both my calculations for WACC and the discounted cash flow model:

My revenue growth rates were about in line with the consensus estimates, however, I decided to bump them up a bit to reflect my cautious optimism regarding the platform's future growth runway.

I also settled on expanding margins significantly, from negative in 2024, to breakeven by the end of this fiscal year, and 13% in the terminal year. This reflects my opinion that the business will benefit from substantial operating leverage and improved cost discipline, as evidenced by my commentary in the financials section above.

Given Reddit's capital-light business model, I assumed modest declines in depreciation and capital expenditures as a percentage of revenue over the projection period.

Based on these assumptions, I arrived at an equity value of ~$7.3 billion and an implied share price of $39.52. This represents a significant downside of ~63% based on Reddit's share price at the market close on Tuesday, May 20th, 2025. While Reddit's long-term growth opportunities remain compelling, I believe the current valuation already prices in all of that upside and more, which leaves zero margin for error moving forward.

Conclusion:

Reddit's Q1 results show a platform that is both scaling rapidly and showing early signs of incredibly disciplined capital allocation and cost controls. The firm's asset-light business model, improving FCF, and new revenue streams all support a compelling narrative for the future of the company.

However, according to my DCF analysis, the stock is significantly overpriced, even using relatively optimistic assumptions regarding growth in revenues and profitability over the next few years.

While Reddit remains a strong competitor in the dynamic and evolving social media landscape, I struggle to see the current share price as a compelling entry point as there appears to be no upside potential at the moment.

For now, I am rating the stock a SELL as I believe investors should remain cautious until the stock begins to trade more in line with the underlying fundamentals of the business.

data.title: Vipshop: Half The Stock Price Is Cash
data.description:
Vipshop Holdings Limited (NYSE:VIPS) knows well how to enhance active users count as well as to conduct marketing operations. Expertise accumulated for offering long-term growth and customer growth from 2008 to 2025 proves that VIPS is well-prepared to navigate a difficult economic environment. I think that investors out there have forgotten the company's long-term track record and the EPS expectations in 2026, 2027, and 2028. The company trades at only 5x, 2028 earnings, with a substantial amount of cash that represents 50% of the total stock price. In addition, my discounted cash flow model indicates that VIPS' fair price could be close to $19 per share with a free cash flow of $800-$819 million in the next ten years. Finally, the cash per share is close to $7.6, and the stock price stands at $14-$15 per share.

VIPS Did Not Report A Beneficial Quarterly Report, But The Company Appears To Be A Long-Term Winner

VIPS reported massive and consistent net income in 2022, 2023, and 2024 from the sale of products offered on the Vipshop Online Platform. In my view, VIPS is a well-recognized business model that will most likely offer substantial free cash flow and net income in the future.

The numbers delivered in the most recent 6-k were not beneficial. The customer count stood at about 41 million. In Q1 2024, the number of users was close to 43 million. I understand that investors may be upset because of the most recent customer growth. With that, it is worth noting the numbers reported many years ago. According to an old 6-k, in Q1 2012, the number of customers was 1 million. It means that in more than a decade, the number of customers in Q1 multiplied by more than 40x times. Given these figures, I am really not concerned about the decline seen in 2025. The long-term growth shown for more than 12 years appears more meaningful than recent declines.

The number of active customers for the first quarter of 2025 was 41.3 million, compared with 43.1 million in the prior year period. Source: 6-k

The number of active customers for the first quarter of 2012 increased by 247.2% to 1.0 million from approximately 300,000 in the prior year period. Source: 6-k From 2012

There is more. If we look at the recent comments made by management, the company appears to be taking significant steps to maximize efficiency and deliver profitability. Before talking about the most recent figures of the company, I would have a look at the following words from management.

We delivered solid profitability in the first quarter while continuously optimizing our resource allocation to maximize growth opportunities. Looking ahead, we remain focused on executing our strategic priorities to regain growth trajectory while investing with discipline and driving efficiency. Source: 6-k

In the most recent quarter, the company noted quarterly revenue of $3.6 billion with EPS GAAP of $0.52, which was worse than expected. In the last 90 days, one analyst declined his expectations for Q2 2025. In Q2, EPS GAAP is expected to be close to $0.42. In my view, we have to look at the long-term expectations, which look much better than the numbers in 2025. EPS growth is expected to be around 6% in 2026, 5.7% in 2027, and 7.8% in 2028. The 2028 PE ratio is not far from 5x, which indicates, in my view, substantial undervaluation considering the company's long-term growth.

Outlook In China, And Expectations From Management

According to numbers offered by the government of China, growth is expected to reach close to 5% in 2025. VIPS also offered some outlook on the company's net sales revenue for Q2 2025. VIPS is expected to report a decrease in quarterly revenue of about 5% to 0%.

China kept its economic growth target for this year unchanged at roughly 5% while committing more fiscal resources than last year to fend off deflationary pressures and mitigate the impact of rising U.S. trade tariffs. Source: Reuters

For the second quarter of 2025, the company expects its total net revenues to be between RMB25.5 billion and RMB26.9 billion, representing a year-over-year decrease of approximately 5% to 0%. Source: 6-k

There are clearly other businesses and other companies operating in China that perform better than VIPS. With that being said, in my view, the company's valuation appears quite disconnected from the reality of the company's business model. The company trades at 3x FWD EBITDA and 7x FWD earnings. The sector median reported by Seeking Alpha is 9x FWD EBITDA and 17x FWD earnings.

Tariffs In The United States Do Not Really Apply Here Because The Company Operates In China

With many companies in China suffering from recent tariffs imposed in the United States, VIPS will most likely not suffer a lot. The company sells pretty much products in China. Clearly, if China imposes certain restrictions on brands from the U.S., the price of those products from the United States may increase, which may affect the demand. With that, the company will most likely behave better than manufacturers from China. In my opinion, investors looking to have access to business models in China may sell certain shares from Chinese companies that sell products in the US, and buy shares of VIPS. As a result, we could see a certain demand for the shares of VIPS.

Potential Decline In The Share Count And Cash In Hand Could Be A Catalyst For The Stock Price

In the last quarterly report, VIPS noted the acquisitions of equity worth US$16.9 million. In addition, the total number of ADS outstanding seemed to decrease substantially. If VIPS continues to buy its own shares, in my view, stock demand could push the company's stock price up.

During the quarter ended March 31, 2025, the Company repurchased US$16.9 million of its ADSs. The Company has fully utilized its US$1.0 billion share repurchase program adopted in March 2023 and has continued share repurchase of US$4.3 million under its current US$1.0 billion share repurchase program, which is effective for a 24-month period through February 2027. Source: 6-k

Considering the total amount of cash standing on the balance sheet and the decline in the share count, the cash per share increased substantially. The current cash per share is not far from $7 per share, which means that close to 50% of the stock price is represented by cash in hand. With the generation of free cash flow and the cash in hand, VIPS does seem quite attractive.

Risks

I am not really concerned about the business model of VIPS or the recent decline in users. I would be worried about potential risks coming from investors in the United States and their perception of stocks from China. If the U.S. Government, the SEC, or any other organization blocks the access of Chinese companies to U.S. markets, we could expect a decline in the stock prices. Under the worst-case scenario, investors could react to detrimental information about such types of companies. As a result, investors may sell the shares of VIPS without paying much attention to its financials. The company is already trading very undervalued. However, under certain circumstances, the stock price could even trade at lower price marks.

Investors in the United States could suffer substantially from declines in the value of the Renminbi. I think that the stock price is connected to the free cash flow generated in China. The net present value of future free cash flow denominated in dollars would most likely decline because of the value of the Renminbi. As a result, I would be expecting declines in the stock price. VIPS gave a lot of commentary in the last 20-F.

The value of the Renminbi against U.S. dollars and other currencies may fluctuate and is affected by, among other things, changes in political and economic conditions in China and by China’s foreign exchange policies. The conversion of the Renminbi into foreign currencies, including U.S. dollars, is based on rates set by the People’s Bank of China. Source: 20-F

I Obtained A Fair Price Of $19 Per Share

The company reported close to $1 billion in total free cash flow in 2020, 2022 FCF stood at $0.94 billion, and 2019 FCF was $916 million. Considering these figures, previous customer growth, ongoing efforts to drive efficiency, and my own assumptions about the future free cash flow growth, I included a valuation of $813 million in 2025, 2030 FCF of $839 million, 2034 FCF of about $824 million, and 2035 FCF of $818 million.

I used a WACC of 10% because the company does not operate in the United States, and I want to be very careful while considering the risks. With a multiplier of 5x free cash flow in 2035, the implied total enterprise value would be $6.8 billion. With a net debt of -$3.2 billion and a share count of 515 million, the fair price would be $19 per share.

My Final Words: The Company Is A Buy At 5x, 2028 Earnings

From 2012 to 2025, the number of customers was multiplied by more than 40x. In my view, the company will most likely be able to offer further business growth in the coming years. Besides, VIPS appears to be making a lot of efforts to invest with discipline and drive efficiency, which could accelerate free cash flow growth in the future. Putting together the total amount of cash in hand, declines in the share count, and the fact that tariffs in the U.S. are not considered a large risk, VIPS could be substantially undervalued right now. As soon as more investors review the company's PE ratio and EPS growth expectations, and execute their own DCF models, in my opinion, the demand for the stock could increase. At the end of the day, VIPS is cheap at 5x 2028 earnings.

data.title: Dividend Yields Vs. Treasury Yields
data.description:
While down a bit today, this week has seen a spike in long-term Treasury yields. Given the surge in yields, there has been a shake-up in the relationship between Treasury yields and dividend yields for stocks. As shown below, currently only 7.9% of stocks in the S&P 500 have dividend yields above the 10-year Treasury yield (4.59% at the time of writing). Additionally, only 12.1% of stocks in the index have dividend yields above that of the 2-year Treasury (4.0%).

Of course, yield dynamics are different for different sectors. As shown below, Real Estate has the highest percentage of stocks with dividend yields above the yield on the 10-year and 2-year. Energy, Utilities, and Materials are the sectors with the next largest shares, whereas cyclical groups like Tech, Industrials, and Consumer Discretionary hardly have any members with dividend yields larger than Treasury yields.

At the moment, there are 40 S&P 500 stocks with higher dividend yields than the 10-year yield, and we list them below. Two Materials stocks top the list with 9%+ dividend yields and over 20% declines on the year.

Original Post

Editor's Note: The summary bullets for this article were chosen by Seeking Alpha editors.

data.title: UPS: Cut The Costs
data.description:
One of the most effective tactics I use is to buy strong stocks in strong sectors. They may have high valuations, but that is usually for a good reason. Occasionally, I employ different tactics when good companies are on sale and the stock is too cheap to ignore. United Parcel Service, Inc. (NYSE:UPS) is one of those stocks. Yes, it has headwinds, and the trade war is a new risk, but at a price of $95, these risks may be mostly priced in.

The Headwinds

Freight and logistics companies are operating in difficult times. Tariffs are likely to restrict global trade, and the US consumer is feeling decidedly gloomy, as the University of Michigan consumer sentiment survey recently posted its second-lowest reading of all time.

However, the tariff story has only come to the fore in recent months and UPS has had problems for a lot longer.

The stock topped in January 2022 and has shed 60%. So it's not just a tariff story. Lower revenues and declining margins have been a driver for several years.

Margins have been declining as inflation has increased costs, and a lack of pricing power has meant prices have not been able to keep pace.

UPS is trying to cut costs with “efficiency reimagined” initiatives, but this is a difficult process for a company that is 117 years old. 2025 guidance was for an operating margin of "approximately 10.8%".

Revenues may have peaked in the post-Covid period.

While revenue growth prospects look limited, they have at least stabilized, and FY24 revenues were 1.5% up on FY23. Q1'25 was -0.7% down on Q1'24, which is not terrible given the trade war in the background.

FY revenues are stabilizing nearly $20B above 2020 levels. However, earnings are in decline due to lower margins and have returned to 2020 levels, taking the stock price with it.

While the outlook for 2025 has been muddied by tariffs and consumer spending concerns, much of this is outside their control. The long-term headwinds are fairly clear and were present before President Trump came to office. Costs need to be cut, and the future of the stock price depends on this key action.

Efficiency Reimagined

Streamlining a 117-year-old company with a massive workforce of 406,000—75% of whom are represented by a powerful union—is a challenge. This was the approach laid out in the FY 24 earnings release at the end of January:

Today the company announces the following set of strategic actions: first, it has reached an agreement in principle with its largest customer to lower its volume by more than 50% by the second half of 2026; second, effective January 1, 2025, the company has insourced 100% of its UPS SurePost product; and third, in connection with these efforts, the company is reconfiguring its U.S. network, and launching multi-year “efficiency reimagined” initiatives to drive approximately $1.0 billion in savings through an end-to-end process redesign.

The stock dropped 15% after that release, so you could say the market wasn't impressed. The "largest customer" was, of course, Amazon (AMZN), and cutting these volumes allowed UPS to slash 20,000 jobs and shut 73 facilities. The loss of volumes is a concern, but they were low margins (even loss-making) as Amazon aggressively negotiated low rates.

Cutting more employees is going to be difficult and the 20,000 layoffs announced in April attracted criticism from the Teamsters union who said, "UPS will be in for one hell of a fight."

UPS may have to focus on other measures to cut costs, but there are no easy options, and that is a major problem.

2025 Guidance and Stock Outlook

Management declined to provide further guidance on the 2025 outlook during the Q1 earnings call.

Given today’s level of uncertainty, UPS is not providing any updates to our previously issued consolidated full-year outlook.

That leaves us with this statement from January:

For the full year 2025, on a consolidated basis, UPS expects revenue to be approximately $89.0 billion and operating margin to be approximately 10.8%. The company is planning capital expenditures of about $3.5 billion, dividend payments of around $5.5 billion, subject to board approval, and share repurchases of around $1.0 billion. The effective tax rate is expected to be around 23.5%.

There is a risk this is guided down at some point as China tariffs have been paused at 30% and volumes are very likely to decline. The company's expectations may be optimistic.

Expect the weakening demand on the China-to-U.S. lane to be partially offset by two factors:

• Growth on China to non-U.S. lanes

• Growth from the rest of the world inbound to the U.S.

The big question, however, is how much bad news is priced in? UPS is down 34% this year. It is 13% below the April 2 closing price (when reciprocal tariffs were announced). The answer is probably "a good deal".

One way we could find out is if UPS stops going down on bad news. That is often a sign that prices are low enough and a bottom is forming. I think that may be around here, as the current price of $95 is back at levels traded at the Covid lows in 2020. As we saw in the first section, revenues have stabilized at a higher level, and EPS is also higher than the 2018-2020 period. Prices are nearly back at levels traded in 2004!

The dividend yield has never been higher.

There is a time when the potential reward outweighs the risk, and that time could be now. My one big concern is if the dividend were to be cut. That kind of bad news is not priced in.

Conclusions

The long-term headwind of high costs and declining margins is being addressed. The company is targeting $1B in savings, although it faces challenges.

At $95, near the Covid lows and trading near levels seen in 2004, a lot of bad news has been priced in. The potential rewards may outweigh the risks, and I rate UPS a "buy".

data.title: SPX Skew Signals Longer-Term Caution Despite Rally
data.description:
Cross-Asset Volatility: Implied volatilities fell across asset classes again last week following the US-China tariff reprieve, with equity and credit implied volatilities dropping the most. The VIX Index fell almost 5 pts while VIXIG (IG credit vol) declined over 7 pts – both are now trading below their long-term averages (see Exhibit 1). Interest rate volatility also fell, with the MOVE Index down 3.7 pts, mostly driven by the front-end of the yield curve. In fact, VIXTLT (20+ Yr Treasury Volatility) increased modestly wk/wk, up 6nms to 127 bps vol as both inflation breakevens and term premium continued to widen on stagflation and budget deficit concerns. TLT skew steepened notably last week, with 3M 25-delta skew (ratio) jumping to the 99th percentile high. Following Moody’s downgrade of US debt over the weekend, 30Y yield is now over 5%.

Equity Volatility: The decline in index vol last week was notable as single stock volatility remained mostly unchanged, with VIXEQ index ending the week just 0.7 pt lower at 37.8%. The 5 pt decline in the VIX was almost entirely driven by a collapse in implied correlation levels, with COR1M index down over 13 pts to 16% (see Exhibit 2). As we highlighted two weeks ago, correlation was the slowest equity risk metric to normalize following the “Liberation Day” tariff pause and it took until the China truce last week for implied correlations to fall back to its pre-“Liberation Day” levels. The options market is signaling once again a return to stock fundamentals with macro risks receding (i.e. high dispersion, low correlation) – how long that can last remains to be seen as bond yields spike on debt concerns.

Skew and Convexity: SPX skew diverged across tenors last week, with short-dated skew flattening on the back of upside call buying while longer-dated skew steepened on renewed hedging demand. SPX 1M skew (25-delta ratio) fell from the 62nd percentile high to the 17th percentile low, driven mostly by the call-side, with call skew (25D/50D ratio) jumping from the 28th to 80th percentile high (see Exhibit 3). The jump in call demand was mostly concentrated in the front two months, as longer-dated call skew remained fairly tepid. SPX 6M call skew, for example, still screens in the 23rd percentile low. This suggests most of the call buying has been tactical upside chasing rather than a positive shift in the longer-term outlook. In fact, longer-dated put skew has been steepening on this rebound, with SPX 6M put skew (25D/50D ratio) rising to the 49th percentile (vs. 1M put skew in the 25th percentile low) Term Structure: SPX term structure steepened notably last week, driven by the collapse in short-dated vols. The 1Y-1M vol spread widened from 0.4% to 3.2% (63rd percentile high).

Cross-Asset Volatility Monitor

Cross-Asset Correlation Matrix (1M)

Cross-Asset Correlation Analysis

Macro Equity Volatility

VIX Index Volatility

US Index Volatility

• The information provided is for general education and information purposes only. No statement provided should be construed as a recommendation to buy or sell a security, future, financial instrument, investment fund, or other investment product (collectively, a “financial product”), or to provide investment advice.

• In particular, the inclusion of a security or other instrument within an index is not a recommendation to buy, sell, or hold that security or any other instrument, nor should it be considered investment advice.

• Past performance of an index or financial product is not indicative of future results.

• The views expressed herein are those of the author and do not necessarily reflect the views of Cboe Global Markets, Inc. or any of its affiliates.

• There are important risks associated with transacting in any of the Cboe Company products or any digital assets discussed here. Before engaging in any transactions in those products or digital assets, it is important for market participants to carefully review the disclosures and disclaimers contained at: https://www.cboe.com/us_disclaimers/

• These products and digital assets are complex and are suitable only for sophisticated market participants. In certain jurisdictions, including the United Kingdom, Cboe Digital products are only permitted for investment professionals, certified sophisticated investors, or high net worth corporations and associations.

• These products involve the risk of loss, which can be substantial and, depending on the type of product, can exceed the amount of money deposited in establishing the position.

• Market participants should put at risk only funds that they can afford to lose without affecting their lifestyle.

data.title: Total Return Breakouts: Palantir, Quantum Computing, And Market Timing Part II
data.description:
Introduction

Quantum computing can leverage algorithms to help us outperform in many sectors of our world. Certainly, algorithms give us advantages to market timing as we review these top picks from December 2024 and discuss the importance of market signals. I have spent the last 35 years trading, researching, and constructing algorithms to identify and leverage value across fundamental, technical, and behavioral finance models. Of the ten portfolio models designed for optimal portfolio mixes for members to beat the market at Value & Momentum Breakouts, eight come from enhancing well-tested anomaly research in published financial journals.

If you believe like I do that there is much more to stock trading than just buy, hold, and hope that every future event works out perfectly as promised for your stock, then these trading models are for you. This article highlights some of our recent top trades from three very different portfolio models, ranging from aggressive short-term breakouts to long-term growth and value portfolios with high dividends.

What I offer is intended to provide a model for future success, as well as build on prior signal events with more insights on how to benefit from changes in the market momentum conditions. Insights may be familiar to long-term readers, but the ongoing sector rotations continue to give us many new opportunities for profit in the years ahead.

Three Trades from Different Portfolio Models

In this article, I provide a sampling of how we use the optimal portfolio models to identify long-term continued strength, and short-term aggressive breakout stocks and even apply timing models to long-term value stocks for the best results. As I repeatedly tell our investment community, it is important to not only diversify your stock selections, but it is also critical to be where the market fund flows are improving for the best returns.

As I detailed in my 2025 Market Forecast article, I am certain that we will see more rotation across sectors, securities, and different trading strategies again next year. Because no one really knows the future and rotations are virtually guaranteed, if you are just riding the buy-and-hold approach you can find yourself in a painful situation.

First, let me briefly introduce a sample of stocks that I share for members at regular weekly, monthly, and annual intervals depending on the portfolio type. Then I will explain why each stock was selected for its respective portfolio. Last, I will share why timing matters even in our value investing portfolios for long-term growth.

  • Palantir Technologies, Inc. (PLTR)

  • Rigetti Computing, Inc. (RGTI)

  • FinVolution Group ADR (FINV).

Since I mention Quantum Computing in the headline, I will include the actual stock by that name as well.

  • Quantum Computing, Inc. (QUBT).

Three Different Portfolios

Palantir comes from our Premium portfolio that actively trades 20 stocks using the MDA cross-sectional financial variables on stocks over a $1 billion market cap. To confirm breakout signals for trading, we rely heavily on the combination of the different variables tested in my research algorithms. The chart below simplifies the statistical model into a colored range from buy to sell signals using green/yellow/red bars. Each bar reflects the strength of the computed values to forecast price change without using the "daily price change" as a predictor variable for what we are trying to predict. In the prior December article six months ago, the model triggered a buy signal on September 23rd at 36.86/share.

Since the last update, we have had several sharp downturns signaled in the MDA chart below as PLTR now moves toward new all-time highs again with extreme volatility this year. By using these algorithms, members avoid the worst of the downturns and are able to have more confidence in buy signals.

Palantir is also in the Technology sector which is showing very large inflows from the April lows. In addition to examining the computation of the stock characteristics, we also monitor the market Momentum Gauges to see if conditions are improving or weakening. Experience has taught us that we can be in an excellent stock with strong fundamentals, but if the broader market outflows are increasing, it will be more difficult to achieve short-term gains. Those signals and trading rules are referenced in other articles.

Next, we have Rigetti from our 2024 Week 50 MDA breakout selection, intended for rapid gains and extremely high positive momentum returns as part of my MDA research. The original breakout signals in November and December last year led to very large gains, but have since shown the stock is more appropriately valued around current levels for now. As the MDA values improve from deeply negative in April we could see more positive signals emerge for this stock and this sector.

Rigetti gained +33.6% in just one day back during the Week 50 selection week and over 206% from the original November 20th at 1.38/share price. The first positive signal showed up in the variables on October 14th at $0.82/share after months without a signal. In this algorithmic approach to trading and investing, the truth is we don't always know the source of investor risk appetite and buying interest. Just as the development of the model was an inductive statistical approach to test over 75 variables simultaneously and see which ones were most reliable in combination with each other — we use an inductive approach to finding what investors are looking to purchase and try to figure out the "why" later.

Quantum Computing Inc., from our Week 48 MDA breakout selections back in November, gained over +60% in the measured week before the positive Segment 6 (green) signal ended as a sign of caution.

Six months later, we are looking back at the signals on QUBT with max negative Segment 2 MDA (red) signals recurring from January through April of this year. However, a green signal has reemerged, and we could see more confirmation for strong breakouts again soon.

Lastly, we have FinVolution Group from our July Piotroski-Graham Value portfolio intended for two-year measurement returns following the scholars' same two-year test period. While measurements are made based on the Buy and Hold approach across the full timeline, we know well that timing matters greatly and signals can improve all your trading.

In the long term, value portfolio we leverage published financial algorithms from scholars like Benjamin Graham and Joseph Piotroski, who have well-documented results in the literature at beating the markets. I have combined their algorithms and enhanced them further to release these Piotroski-Graham value portfolios two times a year, in July and December. Another set of value portfolios for 2025 will be out at the start of July.

Unlike the other two stock selections, FinVolution was originally selected based entirely on the fundamental value variables from two different top scholarly papers. The enhancement of the MDA signals adds to the potential returns and preservation of gains since the July Piotroski-Graham value picks were made for members of Value & Momentum Breakouts.

Timing the Markets

While the value portfolio stocks go out twice a year regardless of market conditions, our other stock and ETF portfolios are very much based on market timing. We made the decision to purchase these good stocks in the periods where they were most positive because experience has shown that fighting against the MDA variables can be very adverse to our returns, regardless of how good we think of our opinion of the selected stocks. This follow-on study is intended to give readers a 6-month perspective on whether timing the market is better for your health and returns than simply using a buy/hold approach through record volatility.

The momentum gauges are based on the MDA characteristics of all the stocks in the S&P 500 (SPY, SPX) to assemble a sort of advance/decline momentum ratio. The Weekly signals of the S&P 500 shown below give us a way to avoid major downturns and profit most when the buy signal returns. We also use the negative signals to buy inverse/bear exchange-traded funds for additional ways to generate profits.

These checks tell us that we are in a good S&P 500 (SPX, SPY) rally, with strong Technology breakout conditions that are highly advantageous to selecting stocks in these sectors. Even value investing can benefit from adjusting appropriately to changing investor appetites, as money always circulates to the next best opportunity.

Conclusion

We are seeing the highest levels of S&P 500 volatility as measured by the number of daily moves larger than +/- 2% than we have seen in the past 2 years combined. The timing signals, particularly daily and weekly MG signals, have been helping us navigate the turmoil as we did back in 2022.

While I don't profess to know exactly why investors choose to buy one security over another, I do know that timing matters greatly and there are some ways to detect strong opportunities early. By leveraging these unique statistical models based on published financial literature, you may be able to leverage our selections even better than I do.

I wish you well in all your trading decisions!

JD Henning, PhD.

data.title: Wall Street Lunch: Bitcoin Breaches $111,000 Mark On Historic Pizza Day
data.description:
Listen below or on the go on Apple Podcasts and Spotify

Fifteen years ago 10,000 bitcoin bought two pizzas. Now that could buy Papa John’s. (0:15) Existing home sales unexpectedly drop. (1:03) Solar stocks get hammered. (3:19)

This is an abridged transcript of the podcast:

Our top story so far, fifteen years ago today, 10,000 bitcoins bought you two Papa John’s pizzas. Now that could buy you Papa John’s (PZZA).

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is setting new highs on the 15th anniversary of the famous crypto pizza transaction that started it all.

On May 22, 2010, early adopter and miner Laszlo Hanyecz bought two large Papa John's pizzas for 10,000 BTC, signaling the potential for real-world purchases and bitcoin as a medium of exchange.

At the time, BTC was only a small development among technophiles and gamers. This morning, it rose as high as $111,700.

So, those 10,000 bitcoins that bought two pizzas would be worth more than $1.1 billion today – almost enough to buy the entire Papa John’s company, whose market cap stands at $1.34 billion.

On the economic front, U.S. existing home sales dipped 0.5% M/M to an annual rate of 4 million in April, compared with the 4.13 million consensus and 4.02 million.

The median existing-home sales price increased 1.8% Y/Y to $414,000. The Northeast and Midwest saw price increases, while home prices fell in the South and West regions.

Pantheon Macro economist Samuel Tombs says: "Housing market activity remains lethargic, primarily due to the gulf between new mortgage rates - nearly 7% right now - and the average 4% rate on existing mortgages."

"The tariff announcements and environment of heightened economic policy uncertainty also have made people less willing to make large financial commitments," he added.

In the markets, Treasury yields are calling the shots as concerns about U.S. fiscal direction mount. The GOP budget bill passed the House by a narrow margin this morning and will go to the Senate.

According to the Congressional Budget Office, the proposed tax changes would increase the federal deficit by an estimated $3.8T over the next decade.

The 10-year yield (US10Y) is taking a breather, trading around 4.6% following a spike in the previous session prompted by a weak 20-year auction.

George Saravelos, head of FX research at Deutsche Bank, says: “The most troubling part of the market reaction (to the auction) is that the dollar (DXY) is weakening at the same time. To us this is a clear signal of a foreign buyer's strike on US assets and the associated US fiscal risks we have been warning for some time."

"It is hard for US equities to stay resilient in this environment," he added. "The 2023-24 period saw a combined rise in US yields and equities as the market was revising US growth expectations higher. This was entirely reasonable. Today is very different."

"It is all a building fiscal risk premium in to US assets. It is hard to make the case that such a (negative) driver of the rising cost of capital is positive for risk assets."

Among active stocks, Snowflake (SNOW) is rallying as analysts praised its strong first quarter and increased guidance, which they say are evidence that its new products and the changes the company made to its sales operation are resonating with customers.

Jefferies analyst Brent Thill said the results were an “all-around clean print” and evidence that new product momentum is picking up, specifically citing Snowpark and Dynamic Tables.

Solar company shares including Sunrun (RUN), Enerphase Energy (ENPH) and SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) are being taken to the woodshed, with the aforementioned tax and spending bill possibly ending numerous clean energy subsidies.

Among the changes included in the were revised language that would end certain tax credits for solar and wind energy by 2028 instead of a slower phaseout through 2031. In the new proposed timeline, solar or wind projects must begin construction within 60 days of the bill's enactment and finish construction by year-end 2028 to remain eligible for tax credits. The legislation also would remove the 30% federal tax credit for taxpayers who install solar rooftop systems.

And for the first time in three years, Urban Outfitters (URBN) reported comparable sales growth in all three banners, including an inflection to positive growth in the Urban Outfitters brand.

This contributed to consolidated same-store sales growth of 5% for the first quarter, beating expectations of 3.4% growth, and contributing to a big rally.

And in the Wall Street Research Corner, Morgan Stanley looked at the latest batch of 13F flings to determine the most crowded hedge fund longs.

Those are the 25 Russell 1000 stocks with the highest percentage of public float owned by hedgies.

Todd Castagno, head of Global Valuation, Accounting and Tax, says: “Crowded trades come with the risk of overvaluation and increased volatility as it may be more difficult to attract the marginal investor, while avoiding overcrowded stocks can provide investors with an opportunity to capture unrecognized value when paired with strong fundamentals.”

Avis Budget (CAR) tops the list with a little more than half of the float owned by hedge funds.

Rounding out the top 10 are Loar Holdings (LOAR), Howard Hughes (HHH), Janus Henderson Group (JHG), Wendy's (WEN), Incyte (INCY), Exelixis (EXEL), Formula One Group (FWONA), QuidelOrtho (QDEL) and Lyft (LYFT).

Check out the rest of the 25 here.

data.title: Lumen: Climbing Out Of The 'Junk Yard,' A Fair Value Upgrade
data.description:
Another valuable Lumen asset sale

It's been almost a year since I had last covered Lumen Technologies, Inc. (NYSE:LUMN). I had been valuing the company based on the value of its' gross property plant and equipment [PP&E], arguing the gross value adding back accumulated depreciation is probably an accurate way to look at the company. Yes, some assets are worth low multiples of EBITDA like the rural copper assets, and some higher than cost like the fiber network.

I subtracted total liabilities [which included pensions] from the gross PP&E to come up with a fair number, a margin of safety if you will, in case the company was liquidated. At the time I was getting a rough value of $5 per share, taking the gross PP&E net of liabilities and cutting that number in half for a possible liquidation scenario.

The asset sale

Fresh off the presses:

Lumen today announced it has entered into a definitive agreement to sell Lumen’s Mass Markets fiber-to-the-home business, including Quantum Fiber, in eleven states to AT&T (NYSE: T) for a total of $5.75 billion in cash, subject to working capital and other various purchase price adjustments.

In essence, Lumen is liquidating part of its' to the home fiber network and doubling down on the enterprise business (emphasis added):

importantly, Lumen will retain assets that will continue to serve as the foundation of its enterprise transformation, including all national, regional, state, and metro level fiber backbone network infrastructure, central offices and associated real estate. The enterprise and wholesale fiber customers will remain with Lumen in all geographies. In addition, Lumen will retain and care for its existing copper network, which primarily services consumer customers.

In reality, it was going to be tough to compete with the larger companies in home fiber that have more advertising spend available, an army of door knockers and Costco "hey, who's your carrier?" sub-contractors out in the field signing up new customers.

However, the enterprise clients seem to keep signing up. The pivot to a pure data center infrastructure play is now a reality with this move. This is the first step to Lumen solidifying their identity and moving from an asset value play to a calculable forward earnings and free cash flow company.

Year to date performance

The company had a big spike up to over $9/share back in 2024. Some of this was optimism on the first data center enterprise signings from big name hyperscalers and some from short sellers getting out [never short a $1 stock]. The share price has come back down to reality and is now squarely back in buyable territory -23% off its' 2025 high.

The goal to climb out of junk status

Now keep in mind that this deal with AT&T is not set to close until the first half of 2026. At this point I will do a rough estimate of what the PP&E assets will look like versus the total long term debt. Firstly, here are the plans of what Lumen intends to do with said cash (emphasis added):

Upon closing, Lumen intends to use the net proceeds of approximately $4.2 billion and cash on hand to pay down approximately $4.8 billion in super-priority debt, reducing our interest expense by approximately $300 million annually. Based on projected 2025 a EBITDA guidance, Lumen’s net debt to aEBITDA ratio will be reduced from 4.9x to 3.9x. The transaction is expected to reduce Lumen’s Mass Markets fiber-related capital expenditures by approximately $1 billion annually, enhancing cash flow and enabling Lumen to accelerate investments in the Company’s enterprise offerings and further strengthen the balance sheet.

Thus we have multiple cause and effect items going on here at once. PP&E will be reduced by about $5 billion USD [I'm making an assumption that the sale price is higher than the cost] rather than $5.7 billion. That would put gross PP&E at $38.54 Billion. Debt would be reduced by $4.8 billion, which is also inclusive of some cash on the balance sheet and sales proceeds. This would put long term debt down in the range of $13.11 billion.

Current total liabilities are equivalent to $33.25 billion USD. With long term debt included in this, the total liabilities would be reduced to $28.45 billion.

Gross PP&E minus total liabilities after the close of sale would be in the neighborhood of $38.54 billion-$28.45 billion [total liabilities] = $10.09 billion USD.

The company has 1,025 million shares outstanding. Thus:

$10,009/1025= $9.76 in gross PP&E per share net of total liabilities.

Should the margin of safety be moved up?

With new leadership in place, lenders placated for the foreseeable future, and a viable business model that has new clients signing deals each quarter, I would say yes. Priorly I was cutting the gross PP&E number in half as a fire sale bankruptcy hair cut, placing fair value at $5/share. Now that I believe the business will survive, I believe the company should trade at least at the gross PP&E per share net of total liabilities. I will not yet value this company based on predicted free cash flow discounting, as those numbers are being vastly reconfigured each time an asset sale occurs and the capital stack gets rearranged, but $9.76 is a fair value in my opinion.

The compounding effect of getting out of the "junk yard"

Highlighted are the superpriority notes from the most recent 10Q filing. While the rates range from 4.125% to 10%, we can assume that the actual rate is fairly close to the high end of this range as an average since this is being prioritized. Here is the average rate the company pays on their debt at current:

  • $17.334 billion long term debt
  • $1.428 billion TTM interest expense
  • $1.428/$17.334= 8.2%.

These superpriority notes the highest priority in the capital stack, was from the "fire sale" days, and getting rid of it should also free up Lumen in other ways as well (emphasis added):

Covenants Lumen Under its Superpriority Revolving/Term Loan A Credit Agreement, Lumen may not permit: (i) its maximum total net leverage ratio to exceed 5.50 to 1.00 with respect to each fiscal quarter ending after December 31, 2024 and stepping down to 5.25 to 1.00 with respect to each fiscal quarter ending after December 31, 2025; or (ii) its interest coverage ratio as of the last day of any test period to be less than 2.00 to 1.00. Lumen’s superpriority credit agreements and superpriority senior secured notes contain various representations and warranties and extensive affirmative and negative covenants. Such covenants include, among other things and subject to certain significant exceptions, restrictions on our ability to declare or pay dividends, repurchase stock, repay certain other indebtedness, create liens, incur additional indebtedness, make investments, engage in transactions with our affiliates, dispose of assets, and merge or consolidate with other persons. Lumen’s senior unsecured notes were issued under four separate indentures. These indentures restrict Lumen’s ability to (i) incur, issue, or create liens upon its property and (ii) consolidate with or merge into, or transfer or lease all or substantially all of its assets to, any other party.

We can see that once this emergency liquidity instrument is extinguished, it can free up a lot of normal business activities as shown in bold. This can set the company back on the path returning capital to shareholders. This is the first compounding effect of getting out of debt.

The next is reducing the net debt to EBITDA ratio down to 3.9X. For hard asset companies like Lumen, a ratio slightly under 4X may get the company re-rated to the lowest levels of investment grade debt. This would give the company cheaper access to capital and not have to rely on junk issues or private credit. This would help the company refinance maturing debt as they come due and hopefully at better rates than their current mix which averages 8.2%. Every basis point counts when you're dealing with billions.

This company is now ultra-focused on the Enterprise client

Interconnecting data centers is now the focus of Lumen's core growth strategy. The company plans to grow the intercity mileage of fiber from 17 million to 47 million miles by 2028. While that is just a "vision," as noted in the presentation, that would mean increasing the size of the network by 2.7 X within 3 years.

Turning this into a cloud based connectivity model can change the scaling game drastically. Not having to wire up each hyperscaler through single ports and having them connect through private fabric ports which can avoid the public internet and decrease latency. This is the goal of Lumen, and they're going all in.

The financial outlook for FY 2025 was reiterated in the most recent quarter. Coming into 2026 after removing the fiber assets being sold from EBITDA, the numbers coming down to free cash flow should even out as CAPEX is expected to drop by about $1 billion to offset reductions in EBITDA.

Risks and summary

I am upping my fair value estimate of Lumen to $9.7/share. The company is turning the corner and hopefully removing some handcuffs with the elimination of the superpriority notes. The pivot into more concentration on AI and data centers is a wise choice in my opinion and getting up to investment grade debt status now looks within reach.

On the negative side, this strategy is highly reliant on the hyperscalers continuing their CAPEX trends. I don't think they will slow down, there's just too many players in this arms race to do so regardless of ROI outcomes in the near term. It will be a narrative however, and any pauses will hurt the share price of anything related to the infrastructure of AI.

data.title: Uncertainty, Tariffs, And Steel Dynamics' Deteriorating Financial Performance
data.description:
In a former time, I traded physical commodities, specifically scrap metal destined for Chinese markets, exporting them from Zimbabwe by way of South Africa. In my current iteration, I have remained interested in the industry, and will, as a consequence, be doing a little series, looking at Steel Dynamics (NASDAQ:STLD) today, whom I give a “Strong Sell” rating, and after, Commercial Metals Company (CMC), two firms I have long been interested in.

A Declining Capital Cycle

Steel Dynamics is one of the United States’ largest domestic steelmakers and metal recyclers, with a steelmaking and steel coating capacity of around 16 million tons. The vertically integrated steelmaker is expanding its aluminum business to further diversify its already highly diversified product portfolio. The aluminum operation supplies aluminum flat rolled products with high recycled content to the sustainable beverage can industry, and automotive and industrial sectors. Nevertheless, the company’s revenue is largely derived from making steel and steel products, processing and selling recycled ferrous and nonferrous metals, and the fabricating and selling steel joists and deck products. The Steel Operations Segment accounts for some 72% of the firm’s revenue, with the Metals Recycling Operations Segment accounting for 24.55% of revenues, and the Steel Fabrication Operations and Aluminum Operations segments accounting for the rest. Consequently, the firm’s revenue and profitability are highly dependent on steel prices, and this results in a cyclical business.

Commodities businesses typically operate within a boom-and-bust cycle, drive by what economics call a “cobweb model”: this is where the time lag between production decisions and observing future prices is high, which increases the likelihood of suppliers creating excess supply in response to rising prices, something which leads to a collapse in prices and returns on invested capital (ROIC), an exit in capital, and market clearing, before the market can recover. The industry has undergone significant consolidation, with the top four producers producing 80% of steel, and producers such as Steel Dynamics, the Commercial Metals Company and Nucor (NUE), tying executive compensation to ROIC. Not only has this occurred, but each of the oligopolists focuses on its own niche, with the Steel Operations Segment’s electric arc furnaces (EAF) steel mills producing flat rolled products and long steel. While this has softened the boom-and-bust cycle, the industry remains cyclical. The reader should keep the chart below from the Federal Reserve Database (FRED) in mind, to understand Steel Dynamics’ fortunes since 2019.

The 2025 Proxy Statement explains that 25% of Steel Dynamics’ 2022 Long-Term Incentive Plan (LTIP) executive performance awards are tied to the achievement of annual after-tax ROIC targets that are set against company-defined primary steel sector competitors, which consist of Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. (CLF), Commercial Metals Company, Nucor Corporation (NUE), Metallus Inc. (MTUS), and United States Steel Corporation (X). In effect, management is paid, at least in part, according to its ability to grow value. According to the company, it has succeeded in claiming first or second across all its LTIP performance measures:

However, despite this, ROIC, by my measure, has declined in 2024 and the last twelve months (LTM), which is ominous for investors: One should invest at the bottom or an upturn in a firm’s capital cycle, that is, when ROIC has bottomed or is rising.

Investing without evidence that ROIC has bottomed, risks earning decreasing, rather than rising economic profits, which translates into poor share price performance. In the last year, notably, the share price is up just 1.59%, perhaps reflecting the firm’s deteriorating economics.

The Impact of Tariffs May Not Be Positive

The standard wisdom around tariffs is that they are good for the producers they protect. This, however, is not completely true. Tariffs levied on imported steel give a short‑run boost to domestic producers through higher output and elevated prices, which fatten net operating profit after tax (NOPAT) margins, with the United States International Trade Commission (USITC) finding that:

Section 232 tariffs reduced imports of affected steel products by 24 percent, increased the price of steel products in the United States by 2.4 percent, and increased U.S. production of steel products by 1.9 percent. U.S. production of steel was $1.3 billion higher in 2021 due to section 232 tariffs.

In the long-run, however, the higher production costs faced by steel consumers dampen downstream demand, and, due to global supply‑chain realignments, lead to diminished competitiveness abroad, lower total factor productivity (TFP) gains and even reduced innovation. Empirical studies by the USITC and scholars such as Lydia Cox show that input tariffs propagate through global value chains, hurting domestic investment, R&D, and export performance of steel‑using industries, which in turn diminishes steel‐making firms’ growth prospects. So, while Steel Dynamics may benefit in the near-term from tariffs, with Steel Dynamics and its peers enjoying rising share prices reflecting anticipated margin expansion, the likely long‑run impact will be a trade‑off between protected domestic margins and reduced market expansion opportunities, as higher input costs constrain capital investment and downstream demand.

Deteriorating Financial Performance

Steel Dynamics’ Q1 2025 results confirmed a troubling decay in the company’s financial performance. Since 2019, revenue has compounded by 8.65% a year, while NOPAT compounded by 8.05% a year. However, as the chart below shows, since 2022, the company’s growth and profitability has declined. Steel Dynamics’ 3-year sales CAGR is -8.21%, while its 3-year NOPAT CAGR is -33.53%.

Since 2019, the company has produced, as it were, some $6 billion in free cash flow (FCF), while paying $1.7 billion in dividends. There is no question that the company’s long-run FCF production lends a great deal of safety to the company’s dividends. However, that safety is compromised by the decline in growth and profitability, given that Steel Dynamics’ FCF production has fallen since 2022. Whereas since 2019, FCF has compounded by -4.37%, that diminution is even greater in the near-history, with the firm experiencing a 3-year FCF CAGR of -40.67%, with the firm producing $619.84 million in FCF in the LTM, with a yield of 2.55%.

Steel Dynamics is Unattractively Valued

At the current price, Steel Dynamics has a price-to-economic book value (PEBV) ratio of 2.66, implying that the market expects its NOPAT to grow by 166% from present levels. Using my reverse discounted cash flow (DCF) model, I analyzed the implied value of the stock based on conservative assumptions about its future growth in cash flows.

In the first scenario:

  • Revenue grows by 6.46% in 2025, 6.43% in 2026, 3.55% in 2027, and 5% thereafter, in line with consensus estimates.

  • NOPAT margin rises to 12% a year.

In this scenario, Steel Dynamics has a market-implied competitive advantage period (MICAP) of one year, with the stock price equaling shareholder value per share in 2026.

If, however:

  • NOPAT margin remains at 6.66%, its current level, and,

  • Revenue compounds at its 3-year CAGR of -5.01%, then,

Steel Dynamics is worth $39.58, a downside of 70.1% from the current price.

If, however:

  • NOPAT margin rises to its 5-year average of 12.48%, and,

  • Revenue compounds by its 5-year average of 12.39%, then,

Steel Dynamics is worth $144, an upside of 5.8% from the current price.

Recommendation

Uncertainty over the firm’s capital cycle and the immediate impact of tariffs, leads me to assign a “Strong Sell” rating on the stock. The range of possibilities that are highly probable makes it very likely that the stock will either modestly appreciate or greatly depreciate in value from current levels. That asymmetry of risk and reward, along with the unfavorable and deteriorating economic fundamentals, inclines me to assign a “Strong Sell” rating to the stock.
